nodes.name: 🔔 Webhook — Trigger Video Engine
nodes.notes: Called by Workflow 03 (Publishing Agent) after product goes live — same trigger as 09A. Also callable manually. Receives: product_id, title, description, category, store_urls, cover_image, brand_id, keywords.

parameters.command:
=# ENVIRONMENT SETUP — runs once per product
WORK_DIR='{{ $json.work_dir }}'
mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"

# Check required tools
echo "=== TOOL CHECK ==="
ffmpeg -version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "MISSING: ffmpeg — run: apt-get install ffmpeg"
piper --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "INFO: piper not installed — will use ElevenLabs fallback"
fc-list 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'opensans\|open.sans\|DejaVu' | head -2 || echo "INFO: Font check"

# Install FFmpeg if missing
if ! command -v ffmpeg &> /dev/null; then
  apt-get install -y ffmpeg 2>/dev/null
fi

# Install fonts
apt-get install -y fonts-open-sans fonts-dejavu-core 2>/dev/null | tail -1
fc-cache -fv 2>/dev/null | tail -1

# Create brand background images using FFmpeg
# 9:16 vertical (Reels/TikTok/Shorts) — 1080x1920
ffmpeg -y \
  -f lavfi -i "color=c=0x1B2A4A:size=1080x1920:rate=30" \
  -frames:v 1 "$WORK_DIR/bg_vertical.png" 2>/dev/null

# 1:1 square (Feed posts) — 1080x1080
ffmpeg -y \
  -f lavfi -i "color=c=0x1B2A4A:size=1080x1080:rate=30" \
  -frames:v 1 "$WORK_DIR/bg_square.png" 2>/dev/null

# Download cover image if URL provided
COVER_URL='{{ $json.cover_image }}'
if [ -n "$COVER_URL" ] && [ "$COVER_URL" != "" ]; then
  wget -q -O "$WORK_DIR/cover.jpg" "$COVER_URL" 2>/dev/null
  if [ -f "$WORK_DIR/cover.jpg" ]; then
    # Resize cover for use in video
    ffmpeg -y -i "$WORK_DIR/cover.jpg" \
      -vf "scale=800:800:force_original_aspect_ratio=decrease,pad=800:800:(ow-iw)/2:(oh-ih)/2:0x1B2A4A" \
      "$WORK_DIR/cover_resized.jpg" 2>/dev/null
    echo "COVER_READY"
  fi
fi

echo "SETUP_DONE|$WORK_DIR"
ls "$WORK_DIR"

nodes.name: 💻 Setup Video Environment
nodes.notes: Creates work directory, installs FFmpeg + fonts if missing, generates brand background images, downloads and resizes cover image. Runs in parallel with script writing.

values.content: You are an expert short-form video scriptwriter for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. You write scripts that STOP the scroll in the first 2 seconds. You understand hook psychology, the 3-second rule, pattern interrupts, and what makes people watch to the end on each platform. You write text-overlay scripts (no talking head required) AND UGC-style talking head scripts. You know that for digital product promotion, the best content is: educational tips that happen to solve what the product solves, before/after transformations, 'POV' style content, and 'things I wish I knew' format. Respond in valid JSON only.

=Write complete video scripts for this digital product:

Product: {{$json.title}}
Category: {{$json.category}}
Description: {{$json.description}}
Price: ${{$json.price}}
Store URL: {{$json.store_url}}
Brand: {{$json.brand_name}}
Primary Keyword: {{$json.primary_keyword}}

Write scripts for ALL 5 video formats:

1. TEXT_OVERLAY_15S — 15 second text-on-screen, TikTok/Reels
   Format: Hook text → 3 value points → CTA
   No voiceover needed — text tells the story

2. TEXT_OVERLAY_30S — 30 second text-on-screen, TikTok/Reels  
   Format: Hook → Problem agitation → Solution reveal → Social proof → CTA

3. TIPS_COUNTDOWN_30S — "5 productivity tips" style, all platforms
   Format: Hook → 5 numbered tips (one per scene) → CTA
   Each tip secretly positions the product as the solution

4. UGC_AVATAR_60S — Talking head script for AI avatar
   Format: Conversational, 'I discovered this' style
   Natural, relatable — NOT a sales pitch
   Include: [PAUSE] markers for natural delivery
   Include: emotion notes e.g. [EXCITED] [RELATABLE SIGH]

5. PRODUCT_REVEAL_15S — Product mockup zoom + text
   Format: Pure visual storytelling with minimal text
   3 scenes: Problem text → Product appear → Result text

Respond ONLY with this JSON:
{
  "videos": [
    {
      "video_id": "text_overlay_15s",
      "format": "TEXT_OVERLAY_15S",
      "platform": "tiktok_reels",
      "duration_seconds": 15,
      "hook_words": "First 3 words — the scroll-stopper",
      "scenes": [
        {
          "scene_number": 1,
          "timestamp_start": 0,
          "timestamp_end": 3,
          "text_overlay": "Main text for this scene — max 8 words",
          "subtext": "Optional smaller text — max 12 words",
          "position": "center",
          "animation": "fade_in",
          "background": "navy"
        }
      ],
      "caption_tiktok": "TikTok caption — hook line + 3-5 hashtags",
      "caption_reels": "Instagram Reels caption — slightly longer + hashtags",
      "caption_shorts": "YouTube Shorts title (100 chars max)",
      "voiceover_script": null
    },
    {
      "video_id": "ugc_avatar_60s",
      "format": "UGC_AVATAR_60S",
      "platform": "tiktok_reels_shorts",
      "duration_seconds": 60,
      "hook_words": "First spoken words",
      "scenes": [],
      "voiceover_script": "Complete word-for-word script for AI avatar. 120-150 words. Natural speech. Include [PAUSE], [EXCITED], [RELATABLE] markers. End with soft CTA — link in bio.",
      "caption_tiktok": "Caption with hook",
      "caption_reels": "Caption with hook",
      "caption_shorts": "Title"
    }
  ],
  "music_mood": "upbeat_focus | chill_productive | motivational | calm_focus",
  "trending_audio_suggestions": ["describe trending audio style that fits"],
  "posting_strategy": {
    "best_time_tiktok": "7-9 PM local time",
    "best_time_reels": "6-8 PM local time",
    "frequency": "1 video per day per platform minimum",
    "first_comment": "Pin this comment with store link for each post"
  }
}

nodes.name: 🧠 mistral — Write All 5 Video Scripts
nodes.notes: mistral scripts all 5 video formats simultaneously. Higher temp (0.85) for creative, hook-driven copy. Runs in parallel with environment setup.

nodes.name: ⚙️ Build FFmpeg Commands (All Videos)
nodes.notes: Builds complete FFmpeg drawtext commands for all text-overlay video types. Handles: fade in/out per scene, font sizing based on text length, accent color for emphasis text, cover zoom-pan for product reveal, brand bars top and bottom.

parameters.command:
=# Render this video with FFmpeg
VIDEO_ID='{{ $json.video_job.video_id }}'
COMMAND='{{ $json.video_job.command }}'
OUTPUT='{{ $json.video_job.output_file }}'

echo "Rendering: $VIDEO_ID"
eval "$COMMAND" 2>&1

if [ -f "$OUTPUT" ]; then
  SIZE=$(ls -lh "$OUTPUT" | awk '{print $5}')
  DURATION=$(ffprobe -v quiet -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$OUTPUT" 2>/dev/null | cut -d. -f1)
  echo "SUCCESS|$VIDEO_ID|$OUTPUT|$SIZE|${DURATION}s"
else
  echo "FAILED|$VIDEO_ID|FFmpeg error"
fi

nodes.name: 💻 Render Video (FFmpeg)
nodes.notes: Renders one video per execution. n8n processes all video jobs in parallel (one per item from previous split). Typical render times on CPU VPS: 15s video → ~20-40s render time. 30s video → ~40-80s.

parameters.command:
=# PIPER TTS — Free, local, fast voiceover
# Install Piper if not present
if ! command -v piper &> /dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Piper TTS..."
  # Download Piper binary for Linux x64
  PIPER_VERSION="2023.11.14-2"
  wget -q "https://github.com/rhasspy/piper/releases/download/${PIPER_VERSION}/piper_linux_x86_64.tar.gz" \
    -O /tmp/piper.tar.gz
  tar -xzf /tmp/piper.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin/
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/piper
  
  # Download voice model (en_US-libritts_r-medium)
  mkdir -p /opt/piper-voices
  wget -q "https://huggingface.co/rhasspy/piper-voices/resolve/main/en/en_US/libritts_r/medium/en_US-libritts_r-medium.onnx" \
    -O /opt/piper-voices/voice.onnx
  wget -q "https://huggingface.co/rhasspy/piper-voices/resolve/main/en/en_US/libritts_r/medium/en_US-libritts_r-medium.onnx.json" \
    -O /opt/piper-voices/voice.onnx.json
  echo "Piper installed"
fi

VOICE_MODEL="/opt/piper-voices/voice.onnx"
SCRIPT='{{ $json.voiceover_script }}'
OUTPUT_WAV='{{ $json.work_dir }}/voice_{{ $json.voiceover_target_video_id }}.wav'
OUTPUT_MP3='{{ $json.work_dir }}/voice_{{ $json.voiceover_target_video_id }}.mp3'

# Generate speech with Piper
echo "$SCRIPT" | piper \
  --model "$VOICE_MODEL" \
  --output_file "$OUTPUT_WAV" \
  --length_scale 1.05 \
  --noise_scale 0.667 2>&1

# Convert WAV to MP3
if [ -f "$OUTPUT_WAV" ]; then
  ffmpeg -y -i "$OUTPUT_WAV" -codec:a libmp3lame -qscale:a 3 "$OUTPUT_MP3" 2>/dev/null
  SIZE=$(ls -lh "$OUTPUT_MP3" | awk '{print $5}')
  echo "PIPER_SUCCESS|$OUTPUT_MP3|$SIZE"
else
  echo "PIPER_FAILED|Will fall back to ElevenLabs"
fi

nodes.name: 🎤 Piper TTS — Generate Voiceover (Free)
nodes.notes: FREE local TTS using Piper (ONNX-based, CPU-optimized). Generates ~10s of audio in ~2s on CPU. Voice: en_US-libritts_r-medium — professional, clear, natural. Auto-installs on first run. RTF ~0.2 = very fast for CPU.

nodes.name: 🎤 ElevenLabs — Premium Voiceover (Fallback)
nodes.notes: SETUP: Get API key at elevenlabs.io/app/speech-synthesis. $5/mo = 1M chars (~100+ videos). Voice ID 21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM = Rachel (calm, professional). Change voice ID for different style. Used as Piper fallback OR for hero content.

parameters.command:
=# Merge voiceover audio into rendered video
VIDEO_IN='{{ $json.work_dir }}/tips_countdown_30s.mp4'
AUDIO_IN='{{ $json.voiceover_path }}'
VIDEO_OUT='{{ $json.work_dir }}/tips_countdown_30s_voiced.mp4'

if [ -f "$VIDEO_IN" ] && [ -f "$AUDIO_IN" ]; then
  ffmpeg -y \
    -i "$VIDEO_IN" \
    -i "$AUDIO_IN" \
    -c:v copy \
    -c:a aac \
    -b:a 128k \
    -shortest \
    -map 0:v:0 \
    -map 1:a:0 \
    "$VIDEO_OUT" 2>&1
  
  if [ -f "$VIDEO_OUT" ]; then
    echo "MERGE_SUCCESS|$VIDEO_OUT"
  else
    echo "MERGE_FAILED"
  fi
else
  echo "MERGE_SKIPPED|Files not found: video=$VIDEO_IN audio=$AUDIO_IN"
fi

nodes.name: 💻 Merge Audio + Video (FFmpeg)
nodes.notes: Merges TTS voiceover audio into the rendered text-overlay video. Uses -shortest to trim to whichever is shorter. -c:v copy = no re-encoding of video = fast.

nodes.name: 🔀 Avatar Enabled?
nodes.notes: Only runs D-ID avatar pipeline if avatar_enabled=true in the incoming request. Set to true manually or enable by default once D-ID subscription is active.

nodes.name: 🎤 ElevenLabs — Avatar Voiceover
nodes.notes: Always uses ElevenLabs for avatar voiceover — Piper quality not good enough for talking head lip-sync. Rachel voice for professional female presenter. Swap voice ID for different presenter style.

parameters.command:
=# Save ElevenLabs audio to file
AUDIO_OUT='{{ $json.work_dir }}/avatar_voice.mp3'
# The binary comes from n8n response — save via base64 or pipe
# This node receives the binary body from ElevenLabs
echo 'Audio saved by n8n binary handler'
ls -la '{{ $json.work_dir }}/'
echo "AUDIO_READY|$AUDIO_OUT"

nodes.name: 🤖 D-ID — Create Avatar Video
nodes.notes: SETUP: (1) Sign up at d-id.com ($5.90/mo starter — 10 min video). (2) Go to API Keys, copy key. (3) Base64 encode the key. (4) Upload your avatar image to a public URL and replace YOUR_AVATAR_IMAGE_URL. HALAL NOTE: Ensure avatar uses modest appearance. Add disclosure: 'AI-generated content' to all avatar videos.

nodes.name: ⏳ Wait 90s for D-ID
nodes.notes: D-ID typically takes 60-120 seconds for a 60s video.

parameters.command:
=# Download D-ID video + add branded lower third
DID_URL='{{ $json.result_url }}'
WORK_DIR='{{ $('⚙️ Validate + Build Video Context').first().json.work_dir }}'
RAW_AVATAR="$WORK_DIR/avatar_raw.mp4"
FINAL_AVATAR="$WORK_DIR/avatar_final.mp4"
A='0xC9A84C'
P='0x1B2A4A'

if [ -n "$DID_URL" ] && [ "$DID_URL" != "null" ]; then
  # Download avatar video
  wget -q -O "$RAW_AVATAR" "$DID_URL" 2>/dev/null
  
  if [ -f "$RAW_AVATAR" ]; then
    # Add branded lower third + AI disclosure
    ffmpeg -y -i "$RAW_AVATAR" \
      -vf "drawbox=x=0:y=ih-140:w=iw:h=140:color=${P}@0.85:t=fill,\
drawtext=text='ClearStack Studio':fontsize=36:fontcolor=${A}:x=30:y=h-105:shadowcolor=0x000000:shadowx=1:shadowy=1,\
drawtext=text='AI-generated content':fontsize=24:fontcolor=0xFFFFFF@0.8:x=30:y=h-60" \
      -c:v libx264 -c:a aac -crf 22 -preset fast \
      "$FINAL_AVATAR" 2>&1
    
    if [ -f "$FINAL_AVATAR" ]; then
      SIZE=$(ls -lh "$FINAL_AVATAR" | awk '{print $5}')
      echo "AVATAR_SUCCESS|$FINAL_AVATAR|$SIZE"
    else
      echo "AVATAR_OVERLAY_FAILED"
    fi
  else
    echo "AVATAR_DOWNLOAD_FAILED"
  fi
else
  echo "AVATAR_NOT_READY|D-ID still processing"
fi

nodes.name: 💻 Add Brand Overlay + AI Disclosure
nodes.notes: Downloads D-ID avatar video, adds branded lower third with ClearStack Studio name in gold and mandatory 'AI-generated content' disclosure. Both halal compliance and platform policy requirement.

parameters.command:
=# Upload all videos to Google Drive
WORK_DIR='{{ $json.work_dir }}'
PRODUCT_ID='{{ $json.product_id }}'

# Check rclone is installed (for Drive upload)
if ! command -v rclone &> /dev/null; then
  curl https://rclone.org/install.sh | bash 2>/dev/null
fi

# Upload each video file
for f in $WORK_DIR/*.mp4; do
  FILENAME=$(basename "$f")
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    SIZE=$(ls -lh "$f" | awk '{print $5}')
    echo "FOUND|$FILENAME|$SIZE"
    # rclone copy "$f" "gdrive:ClearStack Videos/$PRODUCT_ID/" 2>/dev/null
    # Using echo for now — configure rclone with: rclone config
  fi
done

# List final output
echo "=== VIDEO FILES ==="
ls -lh $WORK_DIR/*.mp4 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $5, $9}'
echo "UPLOAD_COMPLETE|$WORK_DIR"

nodes.name: ☁️ Upload Videos to Google Drive
nodes.notes: Uploads all rendered videos to Google Drive for backup and manual review. Uses rclone (free). SETUP: run 'rclone config' on VPS, select Google Drive, follow OAuth flow. Uncomment rclone copy line after setup.

nodes.name: 🎵 Post to TikTok
nodes.notes: SETUP: TikTok Content Posting API requires Business Account approval. Apply at developers.tiktok.com. Review takes 1-2 weeks. TikTok API uses a 2-step: init upload → upload file → publish. Full flow requires additional nodes for file upload.

nodes.name: 📸 Post to Instagram Reels
nodes.notes: Instagram Reels posting requires the video to be hosted at a public URL. Upload to Google Drive (public link) or Cloudflare R2 first, then use that URL here. Meta API handles the rest.

nodes.name: ▶️ Post to YouTube Shorts
nodes.notes: YouTube API uses resumable upload. This node initiates the upload — a second node uploads the actual video binary to the upload URL returned. Full implementation: init → upload binary → confirm.

meta.description: Workflow 09B: Video Engine. mistral writes 5 video scripts simultaneously with environment setup. Text-overlay pipeline: FFmpeg renders 4 videos (15s, 30s, countdown 30s, product reveal 15s) with brand bars, animated text, fade transitions. Voiceover: Piper TTS (free, local, fast) with ElevenLabs fallback. Avatar pipeline (optional): ElevenLabs voice → D-ID talking head → FFmpeg brand overlay + AI disclosure. All videos uploaded to Drive, then posted to TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts. Logs to Video_Content_Log sheet.
